law: Access to Information Act, RSC 1985, c A-1
purposes: To extend the present laws of Canada to provide a right of access to information in records under the control of a government institution.

provisions.text: Subject to this Act, but notwithstanding any other Act of Parliament, every citizen of Canada and every permanent resident... has a right to and shall, on request, be given access to any record under the control of a government institution. (from training data)

Subject to subsection (2), the head of a government institution shall refuse to disclose any record requested under this Act that contains information that was obtained in confidence from the government of a foreign state or an institution thereof... (from training data)

Subject to this Act, a person who has been refused access to a record requested under this Part or a part thereof may... make a complaint to the Information Commissioner in respect of the refusal. (from training data)
Any person who has made a complaint to the Information Commissioner in respect of a refusal to disclose a record... may apply to the Court for a review of the matter... (from training data)

derivation:
MTM-Literal derivation
  m = |purposes| = 1
  n = |provisions| = 4
  purposes served by ≥1 provision = 1/1
  → F (Faithfulness) = 1/1 = 1.0000
  provisions serving no purpose = 1/4
  → X (Excess)       = 1/4 = 0.2500
  α = 0.5, β = 0.5
  MTM-L = 100 · [α · (1 − F) + β · X]
        = 100 · [0.5 · (1 − 1.0000) + 0.5 · 0.2500]
        = 100 · [0.0000 + 0.1250]
        = 12.50
